mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 330) triggers central bank tightening expectations, causing equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive (> 0.5%) in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and residential construction (HOUST) signals robust private sector demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days via improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising oil prices (CPIAUCSL) indicates supply-side shocks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through inflationary pressure and reduced corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4.0%) increases wage pressures that boost consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where UNRATE remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising personal consumption (RSAFS) alongside declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates shifting demand patterns, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sector-specific earnings disappointments.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS rises and HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 200k) in a low-unemployment environment (UNRATE < 4.0%) signals overheating labor markets, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via Fed rate hike expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods where PAYEMS exceeds 200k and UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 102) combined with falling capacity utilization (INDPRO < 105) indicates overcapacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through margin compression and earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO is between 102-105.
